Though an official inquiry ruled the 1985 fire was caused by a discarded cigarette, Fletcher's research suggests Heginbotham's pattern of fires resulting in large insurance claims warranted further investigation.

# Summary

Bacary Sagna, Manchester City's defender, insists he has no regrets about leaving Arsenal despite facing a season without trophies and City languishing in fourth place, lower than Arsenal who are second. Though the 32-year-old acknowledges receiving a lot of comments from fans saying he should have stayed, he states that if faced with the decision again, he would sign for City once more, as he came to join the champions. While Arsenal are gearing up to defend their FA Cup trophy, City's Premier League title will almost certainly be claimed by Chelsea, but Sagna remains philosophical, saying "it's life" and believes the team will return to winning ways and finish the season well.

Nigel Farage has suggested that Prime Minister David Cameron is secretly using hair dye to reverse the greying process, claiming he is "jealous" of how youthful Cameron's appearance has remained while in office. Following their clash at last week's televised leaders debate, Farage said he shook Cameron's hand and complimented his appearance, noting that Cameron has also lost weight and goes jogging regularly. Downing Street previously denied in 2011 that Cameron dyes his hair, though the Prime Minister has been repeatedly accused of visiting stylists to maintain his youthful look.

A 29-year-old Australian tourist, Evita Nicole Sarmonikas, died in March while undergoing a Brazilian butt lift performed by Dr. Victor Ramirez at a Mexican hospital, and her family flew back to Sydney on Tuesday to bring her body home after being pressured to have her remains cremated. It has now been revealed that Dr. Ramirez had lost another patient just a year before—a 52-year-old American woman named Roseann Falcon Ornelas—who died after he operated on her in 2014. Dr. Ramirez's certification has since lapsed, the hospital has been closed due to poor hygiene and unsafe practices, and the Australian Medical Association has warned citizens against seeking cosmetic surgery in developing countries.

# Summary

Help for Heroes has launched a fundraising campaign to help 96-year-old World War II veteran Robert Clark remain in his own home after Brent Council refused to continue funding his live-in carer. Despite Mr. Clark spending his £50,000 life savings on care costs, the council claims it can no longer afford to contribute and wants to move him into a care home, which would be cheaper. The charity has already provided an emergency grant and is seeking public donations to secure Mr. Clark's future, as he has lived in his Burnt Oak home for 50 years and fears that moving would trigger the trauma he experienced as a prisoner of war.

The club's severe financial situation has forced them to take this unusual measure, as they must settle outstanding money owed to avoid UEFA disqualification and relegation. Some players have had their monthly wages slashed from over £7,000 to £2,000 or less as the club attempts to manage its mounting debts.

During this legal battle, which cost British taxpayers hundreds of thousands of pounds, Martuzevicius was briefly admitted to Broadmoor psychiatric hospital but his condition improved by June. Finally, judges at the European Court of Human Rights ruled his case was "manifestly ill-founded" and ordered his deportation back to Lithuania, where he faces charges for 22 crimes including murder, robbery, and extortion.

A 19-year-old victim was also attacked by the gang at a church car park after being lured to meet what he believed was a 14-year-old girl, and has been arrested on suspicion of grooming while police investigate the vigilante group's activities.

David Hibbitt, a 33-year-old bowel cancer patient told he had just 18 months to live, claims he has been cured by cannabis oil purchased from a local dealer for £50 a gram. After being diagnosed with terminal cancer and undergoing chemotherapy, radiotherapy, and surgery to remove his large bowel, he decided to try cannabis oil as a last resort when conventional treatments proved ineffective. He has been cancer-free since his last scan in January and attributes his recovery to the cannabis oil, though Cancer Research UK cautions that there is insufficient clinical evidence to prove cannabis safely and effectively treats cancer in patients.

President Barack Obama visited Everglades National Park on Earth Day to demonstrate that climate change is real and threatening America's national treasures and the economies of surrounding towns that rely heavily on tourist dollars. In his speech, Obama emphasized that "climate change can no longer be denied. It can't be edited out," stating that immediate action is necessary to combat this growing threat. The president called on Americans to make environmentally-conscious decisions to save the Everglades and other natural landmarks for future generations.

Despite impressing in recent weeks with five goals in his last six games, Hernandez's season at Madrid has been largely frustrating with limited starts. With the exclusivity period expired, other clubs including West Ham, Southampton, Newcastle, and Lazio can now bid for the 26-year-old Mexican striker, whom Manchester United values at around £10 million.

After getting partnered with Twitch a few months after she started streaming, Bonnie realized she could earn enough from subscriber fees, donations, and ad revenue to pay her bills and live comfortably. Now working from home playing video games and interacting with others online, she admits she sometimes has to pinch herself at how fortunate she is to have found such an ideal job.

Creators will receive 55% of net revenues from subscription fees, with the service expected to launch in 2015, though specific pricing and exact launch dates have not been disclosed. This move follows YouTube's earlier introduction of Music Key, a music-focused subscription service priced at £9.99/month, which allows users to watch videos offline without advertisements.

Bailey Murrill from Denton, Texas, who had been inexplicably paralyzed for 11 days while hospitalized, surprised her favorite nurse with joy by getting up and walking for the first time after regaining movement in her legs. The emotional moment was captured on video as the nurse screamed with joy and burst into tears while hugging her young patient. The viral video, titled "Love our Nurses!" has been viewed over 1 million times, though the cause of Bailey's paralysis remains unknown.

Julian Assange agreed to be interviewed by Swedish prosecutors at the Ecuadorian Embassy in London to address 2010 allegations of rape and sexual molestation, a shift from prosecutors' previous reluctance to travel to Britain. Director of Public Prosecutions Marianne Ny acknowledged that conducting the interview at the embassy would lower investigation quality compared to having Assange present in Sweden, but decided to accept these deficiencies due to an August 2015 statute of limitations deadline on some alleged crimes. Swedish prosecutors must now request permission from British and Ecuadorian authorities to conduct the interview at the embassy.

Despite his struggles with drug addiction and personal setbacks, Pete is now working on overcoming his challenges and attempting to rebuild his life through acting and pursuing his dream of working in Hollywood.

Borussia Dortmund players showed no signs of being affected by manager Jurgen Klopp's announcement on Wednesday that he will quit the club at the end of the season, as they returned to winning ways with a comfortable 3-0 victory against Paderborn on Saturday. Henrikh Mkhitaryan, Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang, and Shinji Kagawa all scored in the dominant performance, which moved Dortmund to eighth in the table and within three points of qualifying for Europe. The win was a remarkable achievement for a club that had been flirting with relegation earlier in the year, and highlighted the squad's resilience despite the uncertainty surrounding their manager's future.

# Summary

A North Korean defector named Lee Min-bok is sending thousands of copies of the Hollywood film "The Interview" across the border from South Korea to North Korea in balloons, despite finding the movie vulgar and admitting he couldn't even watch it entirely, because he is determined that North Korean citizens will see the movie depicting Kim Jong Un's assassination. Lee launches the balloons filled with DVDs, leaflets, and dollar bills under cover of darkness at the border, arguing that this peaceful method of spreading information is more valuable than military spending and allows him to "tell the truth in safety" to a population where truth-telling can be fatal. The North Korean regime views the film as "an act of terrorism," and their violent response to previous balloon launches suggests that at least some information is successfully reaching ordinary North Koreans despite their government's strict control over media.

An Israeli team of developers has created a thermal protection case called Optimal that prevents phone overheating caused by intensive activities like playing games for hours. The case uses built-in micro-fans to cool the device when it gets too hot and can also gently heat the phone using resistance coils if temperatures drop too cold, while warning users when cooling is needed. This innovative case helps protect smartphones from battery damage and performance issues caused by extreme temperatures.

# Summary

At least 54 people have died and 15 others are missing after a Russian fishing vessel, the Dalniy Vostok, sank off the Kamchatka Peninsula in Russia's Far East, according to Russia's state-run Tass news agency. More than 60 people were rescued Thursday from the Sea of Okhotsk, where the trawler carrying 132 people—including 78 Russians and 54 foreign nationals primarily from Myanmar, Ukraine, Lithuania, and Vanuatu—went down in approximately 15 minutes. The shipwreck was likely caused by a collision that damaged the hull, and the vessel may have capsized due to approximately 80 tons of fish loaded on its deck.

# Summary

With thousands of food apps released almost daily, it can be difficult to choose which ones are best, but nowhere is the abundance of apps more obvious than in the food industry where developers create apps to help you select the best wines, tell you the best places to eat dinner, and sometimes even nudge you in the right direction for meal planning. The article highlights ten essential apps for foodies, including Jamie Oliver's Recipes with easy-to-follow recipes and videos, Love Food Hate Waste with a daily meal planner and fridge tracker, and OpenTable which allows users to book restaurants worldwide. These apps help eliminate the chore of meal planning while offering features ranging from chef recommendations to cocktail deals and comprehensive nutritional tracking.

Jeremy Clarkson continues to battle his career after being sacked from Top Gear, while his estranged wife Frances Clarkson, 53, has been enjoying a family break in the Caribbean, showing off her holiday tan in a white bikini. Discount supermarket Aldi is facing criticism for failing to notify customers of a 0.5 percent surcharge on credit card and tap-and-go transactions, six months after promising the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) it would do so. Despite pledging to place signs at store entrances and registers and instruct cashiers to inform customers of the fees, many Aldi stores still lack proper signage or display it in tiny print, leaving shoppers unaware of the additional charges. The corporate regulator has warned that retailers failing to adequately disclose surcharges may be considered misleading or deceptive under the ASIC Act.

Evidence has emerged of a wartime blunder in which millions of pounds worth of Russian gold and silver, supposedly bound for America as payment for weapons, was lost in the River Clyde. The bullion had been loaded onto the HMS Ulster Queen, a former Irish Sea ferry converted into a battle cruiser, in Murmansk in 1942 and was being transferred to a waiting boat in Greenock when one case of precious metals slipped overboard. The incident, which was shrouded in secrecy and never officially documented, has only recently come to light through a secret diary kept by one of the ship's engineers and now revealed by his daughter in a new book.

Suarez received a four-month ban for the incident despite his initial denial, but still completed his £75 million move from Liverpool to Barcelona. Since his ban was lifted, Suarez has excelled for Barcelona, playing alongside Lionel Messi and Neymar with the team in contention for multiple titles.

He pleaded guilty to four counts of voyeurism and was fined, with a tribunal now deciding whether he will be permanently removed from the medical register.

A former U.S. Army enlistee named John T. Booker Jr., who posted on Facebook about "the adrenaline rush" of dying in jihad, was arrested Friday and charged with attempting to detonate a car bomb at Fort Riley military base in Kansas. The FBI worked with confidential informants to build a fake bomb using nonexplosive materials, and arrested Booker as he attempted to detonate it near the base; the Justice Department confirmed that Fort Riley's security was never breached and no one was in danger. Booker faces life in prison if convicted, while a second man, Alexander Blair, was charged with failing to report the felony and faces up to three years in prison.

# Summary

Swedish scientists analysed over 10,000 families with children aged two to 14 to investigate links between stressful life events and type 1 diabetes development. The research found that children who experience traumatic events such as family illness, death, divorce, or separation could be three times more likely to develop type 1 diabetes. While genetic predisposition remains the most significant risk factor, the study suggests that psychological stress in childhood may contribute to the autoimmune process that damages insulin-producing beta cells in the pancreas.

Nicholson and his friend Jason Huerta are suing the city of Los Angeles for $20 million, with their attorneys arguing that the boys posed no threat and that the officers "shot first and asked questions later," while acknowledging that the teenagers were lucky to be alive. Though the replica gun had an orange tip indicating it was fake, the LAPD claims officers ordered the teen to drop the weapon multiple times before opening fire.

This incident is not isolated, as similar cases have occurred at other airports and train stations around the world, with intoxicated travelers deciding to rest on baggage conveyors.

The series features women isolated in Parisian homes, portraying emotional experiences such as loss of appetite, loneliness, and questioning of attractiveness, with many images showing women lying on beds in deep thought. Through this therapeutic project, Stevens was able to process her own emotions while creating a nostalgic narrative that explores themes of loss, identity reconstruction, and the passage of time.

Contrary to popular belief, Stanton explained that liars often stare and make prolonged eye contact beyond the normal three to five seconds, and he managed to catch both presenters fibbing by noticing micro expressions like a flick on one side of Amanda Holden's mouth. He also shared that liars display other telltale signs such as clutching objects to their chest as a barrier, giving brief answers, twitching, and avoiding detailed questions about their lies.

# Summary

Easter, a unique and major point on the Christian calendar, encompasses the Easter Triduum—three days (Good Friday through Easter Sunday) that represent a spiritual progression from suffering through hope to resurrection, a pattern that Christians and others experience repeatedly throughout their lives. Through the ritual enactment of these three days, practitioners of Christianity are reminded to expect moving through darkness into light, embodying a spiritual progression that reflects the universal human experience of loss, transition, and transformation. This celebration, rooted in deep religious symbolism and paradoxically finding timelessness within time itself, resists co-optation by secular culture and offers a meaningful cause for recognition of the human condition in all its sadness and glory.

# Summary

A nine-year-old girl named Annabel miraculously survived a 30-foot headfirst fall inside a hollow cottonwood tree in Texas and claims she visited heaven and sat in Jesus's lap while unconscious. Her mother, 42-year-old Christy Wilson Beam, expressed amazement that the now 12-year-old escaped the horrifying accident with only minor injuries and no brain damage. Even more remarkably, Annabel was mysteriously cured of a rare, incurable illness that had plagued her childhood, allowing her to eat solid food and drastically reduce her medications for the first time in her life.

# Summary

Bradford Bulls have launched an investigation into reports of car vandalism that occurred during Sunday's Ladbrokes Challenge Cup match against Hull KR at Odsal Stadium. Three cars were broken into and damaged in the Richard Dunn Sports Centre car park, and the club is appealing for witnesses while working closely with Bradford Council, the sports centre, and West Yorkshire Police to get to the bottom of the incidents. Stadium manager Mark Leadbeater stated that they are taking measures to ensure the car park remains a safe place for supporters and will leave no stone unturned in finding those responsible.

# Summary

A mid-air altercation on Oman Air Flight 102 from London Heathrow to Muscat escalated into a "really nasty incident" when two passengers fought over seating, with one man throwing punches and using his shoe as a weapon before being taken away for questioning upon arrival. The article highlights this as part of a troubling trend of "legroom wars," including incidents such as a Delta flight diversion over a reclined seat, an American Airlines flight rerouted to Boston where a 61-year-old French passenger was subdued by federal air marshals, and a United Airlines flight where a female passenger hurled water in a man's face after he refused to remove a Knee Defender device preventing her from reclining her seat.

# Summary

An Iranian military observation aircraft flew within 50 yards of an armed U.S. Navy helicopter over the Persian Gulf in an unsafe maneuver that troubled U.S. military officials, who believe the incident may have been locally ordered rather than directed by top Iranian commanders. During the encounter, the Navy MH-60R helicopter from the deck of the USS Carl Vinson maintained radio contact with its ship and deliberately broke off in a predictable manner to avoid misinterpreting U.S. intentions, with no shots fired. The incident surprised U.S. commanders since Iranian forces have generally conducted operations in the region professionally in recent months, though the absence of the Iranian aircraft for two weeks afterward suggests the local commander may have been reprimanded by higher-ups.

# Summary

A mocked-up image of Britain's ideal dog has been created by combining different parts of several beloved breeds, comprising features such as the coat of an English bulldog, the ears of a King Charles spaniel, the tail of an Irish setter, and the snout of a labrador, based on ideal characteristics voted for by 2,000 pet owners. While the dog looks like an ordinary pet with bright, inquisitive eyes and a wagging tail at first glance, it actually represents a composite of the most desired traits, including a body size similar to a border collie and the soft, round snout preferred by 50 percent of voters. The survey also revealed that pet owners' preferences varied by region, with Londoners favoring smaller lap dogs and those in East Anglia preferring larger breeds like Great Danes.

# Summary

Two soldiers—Scots Guard Shaun Smith, 25, and Welsh Guard Jason Collins, 22, who is stationed at Buckingham Palace—walked free from court yesterday despite a brutal drunken attack caught on CCTV, in which Smith stamped 18 times on his victim's head and Collins repeatedly punched the man's body. Despite being expected to face jail time for the "deeply unpleasant and inexcusable" assault, Judge James Hill made an "extraordinary" decision to give them suspended sentences and community orders after hearing positive character references from their commanding officers. The victim, James Wormald, expressed fury at the lenient outcome, stating that the soldiers should have been imprisoned given the severity of the violence he endured.

# Summary

Sam Allardyce has defended Leicester City manager Nigel Pearson, praising him as his own man despite controversial incidents this season, and believes Pearson has done an unbelievable job despite Leicester sitting bottom of the Premier League. Allardyce notes that Leicester are seven points from safety, albeit with a game in hand against Chelsea, and suggests Pearson must be frustrated because the team's performances don't warrant their lowly league position. West Ham will face Leicester at the King Power Stadium on Saturday, where Pearson's team will look to end a goal-drought and get back to winning ways.

# Summary

In Yuma, Arizona, 18-month-old twins Silas and Eli Keslar drowned after their mother was distracted by a bee and released their stroller, which rolled into a canal with the boys belted in their seat. Despite the mother's rescue attempt, the swift current and steep canal embankments prevented her from saving the toddlers, and the stroller was washed away before emergency responders could locate the boys after more than an hour of searching. According to CNN, authorities warn that Yuma canal paths are popular recreational areas but present serious dangers due to strong currents, deep water, and difficult-to-climb embankments that many people underestimate.

# Summary

Terry McCarty, a 29-year-old American, suffered severe third-degree burns covering 70 percent of his body when he was accidentally engulfed in flames from a bowl of kerosene at age six, enduring 58 operations and cruel bullying throughout his childhood. Despite his traumatic past and the taunts from those who called him "Freddy Krueger," he showed remarkable determination by becoming a firefighter, confronting his deepest fears during training and proving to himself and others that he could overcome anything. From victim to hero, McCarty has since dedicated himself to supporting other burn survivors through programs and plans to establish his own foundation to help children with burns.

# Summary

A fundraising drive organized by Help for Heroes has raised £10,000 in just four days to help one of the country's oldest war veterans, Robert Clark, remain in his own home in North London after Brent Council refused to pay for his live-in carer. Despite Mr. Clark having already spent £50,000 of his life savings on care, the council said it could no longer afford to continue funding the £960-a-week cost and wanted to move him into a care home instead. The successful campaign has now set a new target of £15,000 to help cover the veteran's care costs, as Mr. Clark is a highly decorated prisoner of war and survivor of Hitler's death march.

# Summary

Conservative offices in Aberdeen, Scotland were vandalized overnight with white paint graffiti including a swastika, the word "scum," and a "Q" (allegedly representing "quisling," meaning a collaborator with an enemy) on the windows and door. Scottish Conservative Councillor Ross Thomson claimed the attack was carried out by "nationalists" and posted images on Twitter, stating the party wanted to engage in democratic debate rather than face such vandalism. The incident comes six months after similar graffiti attacks occurred during the Scottish Referendum campaign, and Police Scotland is investigating the incident.

# Summary

Lucas Matthysse defeated Ruslan Provodnikov by majority decision in a 12-round super lightweight bout on Saturday night, landing the majority of punches early and opening a cut near Provodnikov's left eye in the second round. Despite Provodnikov's strong performance in the third and fourth rounds, Matthysse regained control in the fifth by using his three-inch reach advantage to keep Provodnikov at bay and create room to dodge his powerful left hook. Two judges scored the fight 115-113 for Matthysse, while a third scorer called it a draw.

# Summary

Ferrari team principal Maurizio Arrivabene has revealed using a carrot-and-stick management approach to keep Kimi Raikkonen's desire to remain with the Maranello team high, as Lewis Hamilton has yet to sign his new Mercedes contract and is suggested as a potential replacement for the 30-year-old Finn next season. Raikkonen responded to Arrivabene's recent call to raise his game by delivering an impressive performance at the Bahrain Grand Prix, finishing second behind Hamilton's victory and securing his first podium finish in 26 races. While Arrivabene indicated that Raikkonen deserves to renew his contract option based on this strong showing in Bahrain, he emphasized keeping the driver motivated by deliberately maintaining uncertainty about his future.

# Summary

A study by Expert Market examining gender equality in the workplace across 108 countries reveals that only three nations—Jamaica, Colombia, and Saint Lucia—have more female managers than male managers, with Jamaica leading at nearly 60 percent. Although major first-world countries like Britain (34.2%), Germany (31.1%), and Spain (30%) lag considerably behind, the United States ranks within the top 20 at 42.7 percent, and surprisingly, developing regions such as Kazakhstan (37.2%) and Moldova (44.1%) outperform several developed nations. Pakistan was ranked as the worst country for female representation in management at just 3 percent, followed by Algeria (4.9%) and Jordan (5.1%), highlighting the stark global disparities in workplace gender equality despite promising growth in women entering managerial positions.

# Summary

An Australian teenager named Violeta Tupuola, who lives near Canberra, has joined the #kyliejennerchallenge viral trend by placing a shot glass over her mouth and sucking in to create a vacuum that excessively swells her lips to ridiculous proportions. The dangerous challenge, which aims to emulate Kylie Jenner's famous plump lips, involves the airlock created by the glass increasing blood flow and causing severe swelling that can last for hours or even days, often resulting in bruising and painful side effects. Despite the widespread participation among teenagers on social media, the results have been largely undesirable, with many participants expressing regret and social media users criticizing the trend as "dumb" and "gross."

# Summary

Defiant Stuart McCall urged Rangers to maintain belief and show an immediate response after the Ibrox side crashed to a 3-0 defeat against Queen of the South on Thursday night, their second Championship loss of the season. McCall acknowledged the loss was a setback but insisted his players' confidence would not be dented, emphasizing they must bounce back with a strong performance against Raith Rovers on Sunday to keep their promotion push on track. The manager stressed that how the team reacts to adversity is crucial, stating that Rangers will be "fully focused" on securing three points and entering the play-offs with good momentum.

# Summary

The LAPD is searching for the person who tagged police horse Charly with graffiti while he was patrolling Venice Beach with his handlers on Tuesday, with the answer potentially lying in deciphering the cryptic tag "RBS" left on the horse's flank. Despite the broad daylight and presence of his minders, the tagger somehow managed to spray paint "RBS" and an arrow on Charly's side without being seen—a move that graffiti artists typically pride themselves on. The paint was easily removed, but authorities are asking the public for help identifying the vandal responsible for the incident.

# Summary

Queens Park Rangers drew 3-3 with Aston Villa at Villa Park after twice squandering leads, with chairman Tony Fernandes watching the dramatic match on his iPhone from Malaysia. Christian Benteke's hat-trick, including an equalizer just seven minutes before full-time that cancelled out Charlie Austin's apparent winner, prevented QPR from securing all three points despite taking the lead in the first half through Matt Phillips. Despite his despair at the result, Fernandes praised his team's fighting spirit and the point earned keeps the relegation-threatened Rangers in 18th place.

# Summary

Harry Kane tops this week's EA Sports Performance Index after scoring his 30th goal of the season in Tottenham's 3-1 win against Newcastle United on Sunday, earning a Game Index score of 55.1. The 21-year-old, who is nominated for both PFA Player of the Year and Young Player of the Year awards, continues his breakthrough season with 20 Premier League goals. Kane's impressive performance following an intriguing round of Premier League action is followed by teammates Christian Eriksen in second place and Chelsea's Eden Hazard in third.

# Summary

A protective father created a humorous t-shirt featuring his muscly torso with a warning message "Stay Clear Boys - This is my dad!" to keep boys away from his young daughter, though she does not appear happy about wearing the new attire. The image went viral on social media after being shared by Kit Dale, a Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu champion from Australia, garnering over 370,000 likes and 45,000 shares on Facebook. While some commenters praised the dad's creative approach, others criticized the stunt as a way for him to show off his physique and questioned whether the message undermined his daughter's autonomy.

# Summary

A woman's body was found hanging from a third-floor fire escape in Crown Heights, New York on Sunday morning, remaining there for over an hour. One man who declined to give his name told authorities he awoke to discover the woman's body hanging outside his apartment window, saying "I'm traumatized. We got up and she was dead." According to the New York Post, witnesses were deeply affected by the incident, with one passerby initially believing the body was fake and thought it was a "Halloween prank."

# Summary

David Raven, a 30-year-old former Shrewsbury player, became the hero of Scottish football over the weekend by scoring the winner that took Inverness Caledonian Thistle to the Scottish Cup final. After being released by Shrewsbury and Tranmere in recent years, Raven must have thought his career glory had passed when he made only a few appearances for Liverpool as a youngster, including a memorable 1-0 defeat at Burnley in 2005. However, the article argues that while celebrating Raven's achievement, Scottish football is in poor condition and requires major restructuring, with low attendances and declining quality on the pitch.

# Summary

Real Madrid's last victory over Atletico Madrid came in the Champions League final less than a year ago in Lisbon, but since that memorable win, Carlo Ancelotti's side have failed to win any of their last seven games against their city rivals. In the eight months since that final, the two teams have met seven more times, with Atletico Madrid emerging victorious on four occasions, including a humiliating 4-0 defeat for Real at the Vicente Calderon in February. As they prepare for Wednesday's Champions League quarter-final second leg, Real Madrid will be looking to finally end their winless streak against Diego Simeone's dominant Atletico side, having only managed three draws and four losses since their Lisbon triumph.

# Summary

Île Louët, a secluded 19th-century lighthouse island in Morlaix Bay, Brittany, offers groups of up to 10 guests an affordable holiday retreat for €200 (£145/$215) for two nights—comparable to a bed and breakfast stay. Originally inhabited by lighthouse keepers and their families who manually guided boats through the bay from 1857 to the 1960s, the renovated cottage now serves as a unique holiday home, though guests must arrange transport via a local sailor (€100 return) and sacrifice modern comforts like hot running water. The island is fully booked for 2015, but those interested in 2016 can inquire by emailing carantec@tourisme.morlaix.fr.

# Summary

An Indian student, Boobesh Palani, 26, who was studying in New Zealand, was rescued from Wellington Harbour by two teenage girls, Payge Olds and Kelly McKay, at 10:30pm on Monday near Eastbourne after they heard his screams for help. Without thinking of the danger, Payge swam 70 metres out to sea to save the drowning man, while Kelly performed CPR until paramedics arrived. Police posted a picture of Mr Palani lying in his hospital bed appealing to the public for identification, which led to his identity being established so his friends and family were able to be with him during his final days before he passed away at Wellington Hospital.

# Summary

Researchers at the University of Bath and Yale University have developed a new molecular catalyst that improves the efficiency of water electrolysis, an electro-chemical process in which two electrodes generate oxygen and hydrogen from water. The breakthrough addresses the most difficult part of the process—the oxidation reaction that produces oxygen—by minimizing energy losses in the electricity-to-hydrogen conversion, making the process nearly lossless. This advancement will enable hydrogen to be more easily stored as a clean energy carrier from renewable sources like solar and wind power, as the energy from these sources can now be converted and locked into hydrogen fuel for later use.

# Summary

Ed Miliband made his first appearance on the campaign trail in three days this morning in Bristol after spending Easter weekend with his family, while Labour election chiefs have calculated it is better to keep a low profile outside of live TV debates where he can speak directly to voters. While Miliband rested, his political rivals including Prime Minister David Cameron, Nick Clegg, and Nicola Sturgeon actively campaigned, with Cameron undertaking a whirlwind tour of all four UK nations and even tucking into haggis for breakfast in Edinburgh. Upon his return, Miliband launched an attack on the Tory NHS record, arguing that "people waiting day after day for basic healthcare" demonstrates the need for better GP services, though there have been rumblings of discontent in his party over the amount of time he is spending preparing for debates.

# Summary

Saudi airstrikes targeting a military base in Yemen accidentally hit a nearby school, killing at least three students and injuring others, as the conflict has left civilians caught in the crossfire with at least 74 children killed since fighting began on March 26. The airstrikes have devastated Yemen's infrastructure, destroying dozens of buildings across the capital Sanaa and wiping out about a fifth of armored vehicles captured by southern separatists, while the electricity has gone out for 16 million Yemenis in Houthi-held areas. As evacuees flee the war-torn city carrying their belongings, survivors like Professor Damodar Thakur, who lived in Sanaa for 34 years, said they "never felt like a foreigner" but are now forced to leave due to the constant shelling and violence.

# Summary

A Colorado mother, Julieane Jablonski, 38, was charged with providing marijuana to a minor and felony tampering with a witness after allegedly giving her 19-year-old son Austin Essig a pot brownie that caused him to jump out of a third-story window without hesitation in April. The alleged victim later commented on the Fort Collins police department's Facebook post to describe his strange "trip" after eating the brownie. Detectives suspect Jablonski tried to convince her son to lie to police about where he obtained the marijuana after he was hospitalized for the fall.

# Summary

Divock Origi, a Belgium striker signed by Liverpool for £10 million following his impressive performance at the 2014 World Cup, has revealed that witnessing the progress of young players like Jordon Ibe and Raheem Sterling this season has left him eagerly counting down the days until he returns from his loan spell at Lille to make his Anfield debut. Origi credits manager Brendan Rodgers' proven ability to develop young talent as a key reason for joining the club and believes the backing of Liverpool's supporters will help him perform at his best. The striker feels that his challenging season at Lille, balancing league and Europa League commitments, has made him mentally stronger and a better player ahead of his arrival at Liverpool.

# Summary

ESPN sports reporter Britt McHenry was suspended for one week after being caught on camera verbally attacking a parking lot attendant, telling her to "lose some weight, baby girl" and saying "I'm on television and you're in a f---ing trailer, honey." The incident is particularly hypocritical given that McHenry had recently posted blog content advocating for women's rights and criticizing advertisements that focus on appearance alone, demonstrating a stark contrast between her public messaging and private behavior. The controversy has sparked widespread backlash on social media, with a petition calling for her removal from the network, as weight discrimination groups have spoken out against the body-shaming remarks she made during the heated confrontation.

# Summary

Adult colouring-in has become a surprising global trend, with busy mums and grown-ups sitting at tables fully concentrating on colouring books, taking care to stay within the printed lines as a creative pursuit. Scottish illustrator Johanna Basford's colouring-in book *Secret Garden* has sold over 1.4 million copies and topped bestseller charts worldwide, with her follow-up *Enchanted Forest* expected to do just as well. The phenomenon is attributed to people seeking a therapeutic, screen-free analogue activity that allows them to rediscover creativity and escape the stresses of modern technology.

# NHS Spending on Non-Essential Items

The NHS handed out millions of prescriptions for non-essential household items in 2014, including 404,500 prescriptions for suncream costing £13 million, as well as toothpaste, Calpol, and even Yakult yogurt drinks. Critics condemned this spending as "ludicrous," arguing that such items are cheaply available at local chemists and that the NHS should focus resources on essential treatments instead. The analysis prompted concern that patients were abusing the system to obtain free prescriptions for everyday products, while the NHS faced severe budget cuts and was forced to ration routine treatments like cancer therapies and cataract operations.

# Summary of "Skyfaring" Book Review

Mark Vanhoenacker's book "Skyfaring," published by Chatto & Windus, explores his passion for aviation and shares the secrets of his job as a senior first officer with British Airways, aiming to help readers understand why air travel remains an exhilarating and magical experience for experienced pilots. Like the reviewer, Vanhoenacker was enchanted by visiting an airplane cockpit as a child, and that childhood mystique has endured throughout his career, leading him to want us to appreciate the wonders of flying beyond the morbid concerns about plane crashes. Through elegant writing and fascinating details about 747s, flight procedures, and aviation terminology, Vanhoenacker reminds us that we can still trust the men at the controls and invites us to share in the wonder of his profession.

# Summary

Alan Pardew believes Manchester City can still come from behind to snatch the Premier League title from Chelsea, citing their past comebacks including their dramatic one-match comeback last season and Sergio Aguero's injury-time winner in 2012 that sank Manchester United on goal difference. Despite Chelsea currently being six points clear with a game in hand, Pardew insists the race is far from over as his Crystal Palace side prepares to face City on Monday. The Palace manager emphasizes that the Premier League title race is never decided until the final moments and victories cannot be taken for granted.

# Summary

Michael Carrick, the 34-year-old Manchester United midfielder, has revealed that while he wants to continue playing football for several more years by living right and staying fit, his dream after hanging up his boots is to become a Formula One driver, as he is a massive fan of the sport. Carrick credits his longevity in the game to learning from legends like Ryan Giggs and Paul Scholes, and to taking care of himself through gym work, yoga, and proper nutrition. The midfielder also reflected on how moving to London at age 15 to join West Ham was one of the best things he ever did, as it helped him grow and develop faster than he would have if he'd stayed in Newcastle.

# Summary

Wood Green animal charity has partnered with the Handy cleaning and DIY app to offer homeowners a unique solution to mouse infestations—lending them a cat for the day. Cats not only catch mice but also leave behind a scent that deters rodents from returning, and their mere presence and pheromones are often enough to scare away unwanted rodents without homeowners needing to worry about dead mice. This service aims to provide relief to London homeowners hearing the scurry of tiny claws across their kitchen worktops and experiencing the despair that comes with infestations.

# Coachella Festival Fashion Summary

At the Coachella arts and music extravaganza in the California desert, celebrities and festival-goers wore increasingly outlandish outfits, with many featuring exposed flesh, neon pieces, mesh dresses, and skintight leggings. Notable attendees like Jaden Smith, Kylie Jenner, Paris Hilton, and Khloe Kardashian displayed dubious style choices, while other revelers took things to extremes with neon swimming hats, gold lamé outfits, and creative accessories. The festival was filled with bizarre fashion statements, from someone dressed as a full-body mummy in the scorching heat to attendees sporting clashing prints, heavy-duty veils, and an abundance of exposed skin.

# Summary

Sandringham, the Queen's Norfolk estate, removed all 62 hunting trophies including stuffed rhino heads, ivory tusks, and skins from public display on Saturday, replacing them with miscellaneous items and repainting the blood-red walls white. The removal followed legal concerns over unworked items dating prior to 1947, which potentially broke European regulations, and proved particularly embarrassing for animal welfare advocate Prince William, who had previously called for the Royal Family's ivory collection to be destroyed. The museum's new signage credits organizations like the World Wide Fund for Nature, whose President was Prince Philip until 1996, for helping to end this "destructive era" by replacing "the gun with the camera in recording the sightings of these magnificent animals."

# Summary

Prosecco, a sparkling wine from northeastern Italy that residents and tourists in Venice have enjoyed for years, has recently surged in popularity in Britain, with UK sales overtaking champagne for the first time last year at £181.8 million compared to £141.3 million. Though prosecco arrived in Britain about 30 years ago, early attempts to promote it—such as David Gleave's efforts at Liberty Wines in the late Eighties—were unsuccessful, with consumption remaining at just over two million bottles annually until 2009, before leaping to 28 million bottles by 2014. The dramatic rise is attributed to word-of-mouth marketing during celebratory occasions and the wine's affordability, lighter taste, and shorter production time compared to champagne and cava, with options now ranging from budget-friendly bottles to premium offerings like Ca' Morlin Prosecco Superiore DOCG Asolo for those seeking better quality.

# Summary

Dr. Tareq Kamleh, a blue-eyed Adelaide doctor, fled to Syria and appeared in an ISIS propaganda video urging foreign medical professionals to join the terrorist organization, yet remains registered to practice medicine in Australia until September 30 despite the Medical Board's ability to deregister doctors whose conduct goes against being a "fit and proper person." Former colleagues have spoken ill of Kamleh, describing him as a sexually manipulative "creep" who drank to excess and had inappropriate relationships with patients, including an intravenous drug user, though one colleague felt reluctant to speak about these matters until learning of his jihadist portrayal. Immigration Minister Peter Dutton confirmed that Kamleh left on a flight to Kuala Lumpur, and the doctor now faces potential 25-year prison sentence if he returns to Australia and is charged.

# Summary

A tragic head-on collision on a rain-soaked road in NSW left devoted siblings Piper Kulk, 8, and William Kulk, 9, dead within hours of each other, with a heartbreaking image showing William with his arm around his little sister looking out at blue water highlighting their unbreakable bond. The accident occurred when their grandmother's car aquaplaned while returning from the Royal Easter Show with the children and their mother Chantelle, 31, leaving their older brother Daniel hospitalized along with other passengers from the colliding ute. The tragedy underscores the dangers of wet weather driving and follows several other Easter weekend road fatalities, including a two-year-old girl in South Australia and a near-miss involving a couple and their two-year-old son in NSW's Hawkesbury region on Friday afternoon.

# Summary

Italian former policeman Dino Maglio was found guilty of raping a 16-year-old Australian girl after luring her to his home through the Couchsurfing.com website in Padua, northern Italy. Maglio spiked her drink of Baileys liqueur with a tranquilizer, rendering her unable to consent, and was sentenced to six and a half years in prison. An investigation uncovered at least 14 alleged victims who claimed Maglio drugged and sexually assaulted them after arranging accommodation through the social networking site.

# Summary

British politicians including Ed Miliband, Nick Clegg, Natalie Bennett, and Nicky Morgan were asked by Cosmopolitan magazine about sexting, with all advising against the practice—Miliband calling it "not advised for politicians," Clegg describing it as "risky" for young people and "dodgy" if older, and Education Secretary Nicky Morgan deeming it "too risky." Their cautious responses reflect numerous high-profile scandals involving politicians who have been exposed for sending explicit messages and pictures, including former MP Brooks Newmark, Tory official Iain Corby (whose exchanges on gay dating service Grindr were revealed), and American politician Anthony Weiner, whose sexting scandals caused him to resign from the United States House of Representatives and derail his mayoral campaign. The article demonstrates that sexting has caused serious problems for politicians on both sides of the Atlantic, serving as a cautionary tale for public figures.

# Summary

Ian Walters, a 51-year-old church treasurer and driving test examiner, is on trial for the murder of his wife Tracy, 48, who died after he deliberately crashed his 4x4 vehicle into a tree at 84mph on the M1 motorway. The prosecution claims marital conflict over sex and Walters' abusive behavior motivated the crash, while the defence argues he fell asleep at the wheel due to antidepressants; Walters testified that their early sex life was "dramatic" and "very fulfilling" despite lacking the intimacy he desired. The trial continues as Walters denies the murder charge.

# Summary

Manchester Airport was forced to suspend flights for approximately 20 minutes after a potential drone sighting was reported in the airspace around the airport, causing some flights to experience delays and a small number to be diverted to alternative airports while Greater Manchester Police conducted an investigation. Upon inspection, no evidence of a drone was found, and flights resumed after the airport opened its second runway to clear the backlog of traffic. A Manchester Airport spokeswoman stated that while the safety and security of passengers remained paramount, the investigation revealed nothing and normal operations were restored.

# Summary

China's cybercensors, who have long used the "Great Firewall" to block citizens from accessing critical Western content, have now developed a new cyberweapon called the "Great Cannon" that attacks servers outside China's borders, including in the United States, according to a study cited by CNN. The Great Cannon operates by hijacking web browsers of unsuspecting internet users to launch massive distributed denial of service attacks against targets like GreatFire.org and GitHub, which were providing Chinese readers access to uncensored news and ways to circumvent the firewall. Researchers warn that this more visible and aggressive attack capability represents a significant escalation in China's efforts to suppress free speech and international access to information.

# Summary

Ciudad Real Airport in Spain, built in 2009 at a cost of over €1 billion during Spain's economic boom, closed just three years later due to financial difficulties and is now being sold for as little as one pound. Originally designed to serve Madrid and the Andalusian coast with an expected 5 million passengers annually, the airport failed to attract airlines after its last commercial flight in October 2011 and now stands as an abandoned "white elephant" symbol of Spain's economic downturn. The new owners will inherit hundreds of millions of pounds in debt, though the airport still requires ongoing maintenance including painting yellow crosses on its runway to prevent unauthorized landings.

# Summary

An NYPD detective named Patrick Cherry was caught on video screaming at and verbally abusing an Uber driver named Humayun after the driver honked at him for not signaling while parking, with the incident viewed over three million times on YouTube and sparking mass outcry. Following the widespread public reaction, Detective Cherry appeared on NBC 4 New York to apologize, stating "I sincerely apologize" and acknowledging that "people shouldn't be treated that way," while admitting he let his emotions get the better of him. In response to the incident, Cherry was stripped of his shield and gun, placed on administrative duty, and transferred out of the Joint Terrorism Task Force, with NYPD Police Chief Bill Bratton condemning the behavior as unacceptable and stating that it made all good cops' jobs harder.

# Summary

Former Manchester United goalkeeper Edwin van der Sar is convinced that manager Louis van Gaal will bring the glory days back to Old Trafford, as United head to Chelsea on Saturday in top form after winning six successive matches. While van der Sar admits the Premier League title is probably beyond United's grasp this season due to Chelsea's dominance and their game in hand, he believes the club can challenge for the championship next year with the right summer signings. Van Gaal is reportedly planning to strengthen the squad with acquisitions like Mats Hummels, Nathaniel Clyne, and Kevin Strootman to help United revive its fortunes.

# Summary

Bournemouth and Sheffield Wednesday played out a dramatic 2-2 draw at Dean Court that perfectly represented an absurd season, with the match seeing Bournemouth trail to Kieran Lee's goal, level through Yann Kermorgant, and have a man sent off—yet still manage to go ahead through Matt Ritchie's free-kick before Chris Maguire converted a late penalty to salvage a point for Wednesday. Eddie Howe was left heartbroken and caught between regret and unhappiness with referee Paul Tierney, feeling his side had been denied a penalty when Callum Wilson was fouled and suggesting that referees had conspired against Bournemouth after the team received 15 penalties this season. The result proved costly for Bournemouth's title hopes, as they squandered a major opportunity to maintain their push toward the top flight.

# Summary

An 18-year-old woman, Billy-Anne Huxham, was kidnapped at knifepoint by her ex-boyfriend, Carl Garry Chapman (32), from her home in Caboolture, Queensland early on Thursday morning. After escaping and making contact with a family member, police located her at 9.45pm on Aerodrome Road in Maroochydore after finding the Nissan SUV used in her abduction. Chapman has been charged with multiple offences including assault, torture, deprivation of liberty, and dangerous conduct with a weapon.

# Southampton's Scoring Struggles

Ronald Koeman has sent a clear message to his Southampton players to forget about others and focus on themselves as the team's stellar season has stuttered in recent weeks due to their inability to score goals. In their last 10 league matches, the Saints have scored just seven times, earning only 14 points from a possible 30, prompting Koeman to work on improving their attacking quality and chance creation. Despite these struggles, Southampton remain seventh in the Premier League with 53 points and are still in contention for European football, starting with a crucial home match against Hull City on Saturday.

# Summary

Teller Lake in Boulder, Colorado has become invaded by thousands of goldfish—believed to number between 3,000 and 4,000—after someone dumped just four or five of their pets into the water two or three years ago. These non-native goldfish are threatening the lake's natural species by competing for resources and spreading unnatural diseases, while also eating up vital food sources. Colorado Parks and Wildlife officials warn that dumping pets into lakes can bring diseases to native animals and plants, and are considering using electrofishing to remove the invasive goldfish from the 12-acre lake.

# Summary

Tim Howard adamantly rejects the notion that Europa League participation is to blame for Everton's disappointing season, which could be their worst in nine years and might see them finish in the bottom half of the Premier League for the first time since 2006. Despite a four-game unbeaten run, Roberto Martinez's side has fallen significantly below last season's standards, with Howard arguing that citing European competition as an excuse is merely conventional wisdom that doesn't hold up when other teams face similar demands. After ensuring his pregnant partner was safe while clinging to the overturned hull, the man tied a rope around himself and dove beneath the vessel to locate the flare to alert rescuers. Both were quickly rescued by the RNLI lifeboat and Coastguard after a member of the public spotted them in the water off Kent, and they were taken to hospital where they were confirmed unharmed despite being cold and shaken.

# Summary

A 17-year-old Connecticut teen named Cassandra C. who was diagnosed with Hodgkin's lymphoma in September completed her chemotherapy treatment and left the hospital Monday after being held in state custody since December. Doctors had given her an 85% chance of survival if treated, and though she started treatment in November and ran away after two days, a judge ordered her into state custody to continue the therapy. Attorneys for the teen and her mom tried to appeal and her attorney made a request to allow her return home, but these efforts were unsuccessful until her treatment was complete and she entered remission.

# Summary

Pierre Collins, the father of 10-year-old Barway Collins whose body was found in the Mississippi River over the weekend, was arrested Monday on suspicion of second-degree murder—less than a month after he tearfully pleaded for his son's safe return at a vigil. Collins was taken into custody based on electronic evidence showing he was in the area of the river at the time his son disappeared on March 18. The boy's body was discovered Saturday by Boy Scout searchers about ten feet from the river's edge in Brooklyn Center, and authorities have determined his death to be a homicide.

# Summary

Following Saturday's devastating earthquake that killed more than 4,000 people, Nepal has begun the grim task of mass cremations along the Bagmati river that divides Kathmandu, as mourning families attempt to give their loved ones an honorable Hindu send-off. Today, plumes of white smoke could be seen floating across the capital as hundreds of bodies were burned in the ghats beside the river, with mourning families gathered around the ceremonies wailing in grief for their lost loved ones. Despite the overwhelming number of victims, the shortage of wood needed for pyres and limited cremation space have forced families to conduct last rites wherever they can find room along the banks.

# Summary

A horse named Cody got stuck in thick mud in a bog in Belvedere, south-east London, on Friday afternoon, possibly distracted by the excitement of the Grand National. Animal rescue experts spent over an hour working to free the distressed animal from the watery ditch and drag him to safety. Although Cody was covered head to hoof in mud and shaken by the ordeal, he was otherwise unharmed and was taken to the stables to be examined by a vet.

# Summary

Karen Bell, a 42-year-old mother from Hull, East Yorkshire, has been jailed for 12 months after blackmailing a man she met on the dating website Plenty of Fish. Bell threatened to post videos of their consensual sexual encounter online unless the man paid her money, initially demanding £25 before increasing her demands to £55. She pleaded guilty to blackmail at Hull Crown Court, claiming her son had secretly filmed their tryst and would publish it on Facebook and local websites if payment was not made.

# Summary

Following his inspired charity work in Africa, 37-year-old Didier Drogba has been awarded the Barclays Spirit of the Game trophy for his efforts to inspire the next generation of footballers to fall in love with the game through his foundation. The Chelsea forward, who scored the equaliser against Leicester on Wednesday, spoke about his passion for helping children in his home country of Ivory Coast achieve their dreams through education and healthcare support. Chelsea stars recently raised £400,000 at a charity ball to help complete a medical clinic in Abidjan and fund mobile clinics for those unable to access healthcare.

# Summary

Mohammad Qamaruzzaman, assistant secretary general of Bangladesh's Jamaat-e-Islami party, was executed on Saturday inside a jail in Dhaka after being convicted of crimes against humanity. He headed a militia group that collaborated with the Pakistani army during the 1971 independence war and was responsible for the killing of around 120 unarmed farmers, according to prosecutors. His execution has drawn criticism from supporters who called for nationwide protests, and international human rights organizations also opposed the move, claiming the trial did not meet fair international standards.

# Summary

The author visits iconic political locations like the Capital Grille and Capitol Hill, learning how Washington's real political landscape mirrors the show's dark power-broking and intrigue, while also highlighting the city's major tourist attractions including the Smithsonian museums and National Archives. The article concludes with travel information, offering four nights at the Loews Madison Hotel from £799 per person through America As You Like It (020 8742 8299).

# Summary

Eight-year-old Leo Bernal is fortunate to have survived after an armed intruder shot him in the head as he slept in his Culver City, California home over the weekend. Released from the hospital on Friday, five days after the shooting, Leo underwent surgery to have his skull opened and the bullet removed, leaving him with 23 staples holding his scalp together. The gunman, who opened fire at around 11:55 p.m. on Sunday while chasing Leo's father through the home, remains at large, and police are asking the public for help in locating him.

# Summary

A suicide bomber detonated explosives near a group of protesters in Khost, eastern Afghanistan on Thursday, killing 17 people and wounding 64 others, according to police. Among the wounded was Humayoon Humayoon, an Afghan member of parliament, who was participating in protests against the provincial governor. The Taliban denied responsibility for the attack, and no other organization has claimed it, according to CNN reporting.

# Summary

According to CNN, the third World Happiness Report released by the Sustainable Development Solutions Network for the United Nations revealed that people living in the happiest countries enjoy longer life expectancies, more social support, greater generosity, more freedom to make life choices, and higher gross domestic products per capita. Switzerland topped the list of the world's 10 happiest places, followed by Iceland and Denmark, with each country offering unique attractions such as finest Swiss chocolate, geothermal hot springs, and the Danish tradition of "hygge." The report encourages governments worldwide to measure and improve their people's happiness by recognizing that well-being depends on multiple factors beyond wealth, including trust in society, low corruption, and community generosity.

# Summary

Four-year-old Austin West drowned in his family's backyard pool last week after slipping while chasing a bumblebee, and when his grandfather Jeff West attempted to set up a GoFundMe page to help raise money towards the boy's funeral and final expenses, he discovered that scammers had beat him to the punch by creating multiple fraudulent fundraisers. West lamented that he doesn't know how many people donated to these phony accounts before the real page was established, saying "What people will do to make a buck off of a child." The West family is now trying to raise $10,000 through legitimate channels, including their authentic GoFundMe campaign and selling cotton T-shirts with Austin's picture on Booster.com.

An 86-year-old Jean-Marie Le Pen, founder of France's National Front, withdrew from regional elections after sparking a family rift with racist and anti-Semitic remarks, including calling the Holocaust "a detail of history." His daughter Marine, the party's current leader, furiously opposed his candidacy, accusing him of "political suicide." Le Pen announced he would step aside in favor of his 25-year-old granddaughter Marion Marechal-Le Pen to prevent further damage to the far-right party.

Andrew Getty, the 47-year-old grandson of oil tycoon J. Paul Getty, died Tuesday afternoon at his Los Angeles home, with authorities stating his death "appears to be natural (causes) or an accident." His parents, Ann and Gordon Getty, released a statement requesting that members of the media and public respect the family's privacy during this difficult time. Some medication was recovered from Getty's home, though investigators are uncertain whether he was currently taking it or details about his medical history.

# Summary

Cricket photography from around the world is showcased in the shortlist for the Wisden–MCC Cricket Photograph of the Year 2014, demonstrating the sport's truly global reach through images of children, amateurs, and professional players. The winning photograph by Getty Images photographer Matthew Lewis captures West Indies player Dwayne Bravo taking a breathtaking full-length catch to dismiss Australia's James Faulkner during last year's ICC World Twenty20 competition in Bangladesh. Beyond this stunning professional moment, the shortlist also features portraits of ordinary players, ranging from children playing cricket on a rubbish tip in Bangladesh to amateur club players attempting catches in Kent.

# Summary

A Reddit thread asking users to share the most horrendous baby names they've encountered attracted over 18,000 comments in just 24 hours, revealing bizarre naming choices that raise questions about parental judgment. The thread uncovered shocking examples including creative misspellings like "Airwrecker" (an alternative spelling of Erica), unusual combinations like "Vejonica" (merging John and Veronica), and outright questionable names such as "Orgasm," "Boy Boy," and "Abstinence." These submissions highlight the sometimes questionable decisions parents make when naming their children, with some appearing designed to reference celebrities or trending topics rather than considering the child's future.

# Hayfever Surge Expected in UK This Summer

The number of hayfever sufferers in the UK is predicted to reach 20 million this summer, up a third from last year, as warm weather bathes the country. While many welcome the promise of temperatures warmer than the Mediterranean this week, millions face impending misery from a dreaded cloud of invisible pollen. Experts warn that five million people are expected to experience hayfever symptoms for the first time, with the figure projected to surge to 31.8 million by 2030 due to climate change and lengthening pollen seasons.

# Summary

Raheem Sterling, Liverpool's 20-year-old forward, has rejected a £100,000-per-week contract offer from the club and postponed negotiations until summer, confident in his market value with two years remaining on his current £35,000-per-week deal. The article traces Sterling's remarkable journey from a tough Jamaican community to becoming one of England's most promising talents, having progressed through Queens Park Rangers' academy before joining Liverpool at age 15 for £500,000. Despite his youth and past off-field issues, Sterling has established himself as a key player for both Liverpool and England, positioning himself to potentially secure one of the club's most lucrative contracts.

TV star Karren Brady, the government's small business ambassador, was hoping to steal some of the limelight from Prime Minister David Cameron at an event announcing 16,000 new apprenticeships, but was instead relegated to coat duty, helping Mr. Cameron with his jacket as he stood to speak before sitting politely on the sidelines. The businesswoman and Apprentice judge appeared alongside Cameron at a National Grid training centre in Newark to announce major commitments from companies including Morrisons (9,000 apprentices), Costa Coffee, and the National Grid (500 apprentices). Cameron reiterated his pledge to create three million more apprenticeships over the next five years if the Conservative Party wins the election, emphasizing his goal to make apprenticeships "level-pegging" with university degrees.

Daredevil skier Oscar Hübinette decided to take extreme skiing to a whole new level by hitting the slopes on the active Tolbachik volcano in Russia's Kamchatka Peninsula, while adventure photographer Fredrik Schenholm looked on in amazement. As Oscar flew down the icy snow at -25°C, he was silhouetted against the volcano's lava erupting at over 1,000°C, creating a stunning contrast of black and red lava against white snow. After chasing this shot for five years across multiple continents, Fredrik finally captured the perfect image of the daredevil skier performing death-defying runs on the still-active volcano.

Newcastle head coach John Carver says he will "use a whip" to motivate his players ahead of Sunday's Tyne-Wear derby at the Stadium of Light, acknowledging that Sunderland have out-worked Newcastle in their last four encounters with their North-East rivals. Carver insists that Newcastle have plenty to prove despite their mid-table position, emphasizing that players should be motivated to play in any Premier League match, and has named Moussa Sissoko as captain for the clash. The squad will include midfielder Mehdi Abeid, though defender Massadio Haidara is ruled out for the season after knee surgery.

The Scottish Football Association failed in their attempt to increase the punishment for Rangers goalkeeper Steve Simonsen, 35, who admitted to breaching betting laws by wagering on 50 games over a year and received a one-game ban. SFA compliance officer Tony McGlennan challenged the decision, claiming the punishment was unduly lenient, but an appellate tribunal rejected the SFA's appeal to impose a harsher sentence. Simonsen's ban was less severe than previous cases involving players who bet on matches in which they were directly involved.

While critics argue that such friendliness during an intense local derby is inappropriate, the author contends that condemning Krul for acknowledging a superb strike reflects a troubling loss of humanity in modern football. The piece suggests that the criticism may also reflect bias, noting that an English player showing similar sportsmanship would likely be praised as a "heart-warming example" rather than condemned.

# Summary

President Barack Obama and his wife Michelle paid more than $93,000 in federal taxes last year on an adjusted gross income of over $477,000, with an effective tax rate of 19.6 percent, according to tax returns released Friday by the White House. The couple claimed nearly $160,000 in itemized deductions, including $70,712 in charitable donations to 33 different charities, with their largest donation being $22,012 to the Fisher House Foundation, which supports military families. Altogether, the Obamas donated nearly 15 percent of their income to various charitable organizations.

# Saracens Sign Marcelo Bosch to Contract Extension

Saracens have announced that 31-year-old Argentina centre Marcelo Bosch has signed a contract extension at Allianz Park. Bosch was the hero of Saracens' European Champions Cup quarter-final victory over Racing Metro, booting a last-minute penalty with the game's final kick to secure a 12-11 success in Paris. The international, who joined Saracens from Biarritz in 2013, has earned praise from rugby director Mark McCall for his hard work and dedication to improving his game.

# Summary

Human rights lawyer Amal Clooney held a press conference in Washington DC today calling for the release of imprisoned former Maldivian president Mohamed Nasheed, accompanied by his wife Laila Ali and Jared Genser, founder of Freedom Now. Nasheed, who came to power in 2008 as the Maldives' first democratically elected leader, was convicted under anti-terror laws last month and sentenced to 13 years in prison in what international observers have called a politically motivated trial. Clooney pledged to appeal to the U.S. government to pressure the Maldivian government to secure her client's release.

# Paula Radcliffe's London Marathon Farewell

Paula Radcliffe, a legendary British runner who won the London Marathon three times and set an unbeaten world record of 2:15:25 in 2003, will run the race one final time this weekend as part of her retirement from professional running. Despite battling back from injuries and never winning an Olympic medal—often being out-sprinted in the final moments at major championships—Radcliffe became an inspiration to elite female athletes by proving that motherhood and a successful running career could be combined. After receiving numerous GB vests throughout her career, earning an MBE, and being voted BBC Sports Personality of the Year in 2002, the 41-year-old will now participate in the race with the masses rather than as an elite competitor, and will receive the inaugural John Disley Lifetime Achievement award in recognition of her remarkable achievements.

Ramsey reflected on the emotional toll of losing his grandmother, recounting how she was a significant influence in his life and always believed in him throughout his football career. The Welsh midfielder also discussed Arsenal's FA Cup victory the previous season and their current pursuit of the Premier League title, expressing optimism about the team's momentum and chances despite Chelsea's lead.

Rather than use simple tricks like a whoopie cushion, Lily took significant steps to make the prank convincing, pretending that an intruder had taken her and demanding a £4,000 ransom. Although her mother did not find the joke amusing, Lily's prank went viral on Twitter, receiving over 18,000 retweets after she posted the exchange online.

The current influx has increased dramatically following the overthrow of Gadhafi, whose regime previously controlled migrant departures from Libya, and Italy's subsequent ending of its Mare Nostrum rescue program in 2014. Despite supposed fingerprinting requirements, many migrants arrive without proper processing and eventually disperse across Europe, with some like IT specialist Moutassem Yazbek making it to Germany to seek better opportunities.

Although West Ham have struggled with just 11 points since the turn of the year, Allardyce emphasized that the season as a whole has been a success, as the club has already exceeded last year's total by two points with seven games to spare. Allardyce acknowledged that recent results have been disappointing but maintained that performances have been strong and suggested that fans shouldn't overreact based on just a few recent games.

A new study from Edge Hill University has found that exam anxiety directly impacts student performance, with worried pupils more likely to achieve lower grades. To help students succeed during exam season, experts recommend creating a structured revision timetable with breaks and rewards, finding a distraction-free study space, using practice questions and revision aids like flashcards, and trying new techniques such as "Spaced Learning." Parents play a crucial role by staying engaged, testing their children, maintaining a positive attitude, and ensuring they eat well and exercise regularly.

With Scott and potentially back-row forward David Denton sidelined, Edinburgh face Newport Gwent Dragons in Friday's semi-final, though the Dragons may be in the lower half of the table, they are in excellent form, having taken their biggest scalp last weekend by defeating Leinster 25-22. Despite these challenges, hooker Ross Ford remains determined to lead Edinburgh to the Challenge Cup Final at Twickenham, which he believes would be his greatest achievement and a significant boost for Scottish rugby.

The UN's World Meteorological Organization removed the name Isis from its official list of Pacific hurricane names on Friday because the name is associated with the Islamic State militant group that has committed atrocities in Iraq and Syria. The Hurricane Committee agreed to replace Isis with Ivette on the rotating list, which cycles every six years and runs from A to Z alternating between male and female names. Names are only removed from the list if they are deemed inappropriate or if they caused significant damage and death, though this was not the case with Isis.

# Game of Thrones Season 5 Episodes Leaked Online

Game of Thrones fans who did not want to wait have leaked the first four episodes of the show's fifth season online, with the episodes beginning to appear on torrent sites Saturday night, likely from review copies given to the press. The episodes, which follow competing noble factions and families in the fantasy world of Westeros, had been downloaded over 550,000 times by early Sunday morning. Fans of the series now face the challenge of avoiding spoilers online as the episodes circulate before the official premiere airs tonight.

More than half of UK voters oppose the SNP calling the shots in the next British government, with 59 percent wanting the party to play no role entirely, according to a new ComRes poll. While the SNP and Labour have ruled out a formal coalition with SNP MPs sitting in Cabinet, they have left open the possibility of the SNP supporting a Labour government on a vote-by-vote basis, with the SNP already suggesting it will be "entitled to vote against any bit of legislation" it does not agree with. Key election promises in the SNP manifesto include demands for extra spending, an end to benefits cuts, and the scrapping of Trident, as Nicola Sturgeon launches her party's bid to make huge gains in Scotland amid expectations of another hung parliament.

The experiment revealed the collection's universal appeal, as the bright pink, girly outfits sparked smiles, opened doors, and generated significant social media attention from everyone she encountered. Doshay was arrested last week after allegedly attempting to kidnap a 7-year-old girl from Skyline Elementary School in Solana Beach on March 23, grabbing her and trying to cover her mouth with packing tape before fleeing when teachers and parents heard her screams. Sangakkara announced his retirement from limited overs cricket on March 18, disappointed after Sri Lanka's World Cup quarter-final defeat, though he noted the country was in a better place than when he joined the side in 2000. The 37-year-old batsman, who will continue playing Tests, has accumulated 14,234 one-day runs and scored a record four successive centuries in the World Cup before bowing out.

Speaking at Westerton Care Home during a campaign visit, Miss Sturgeon said the SNP demanded the retirement age be frozen and called for a huge inflation-busting increase in the flat rate pension to at least £160 a week. The SNP's demands would cost billions of pounds but would be offered as the price of propping up Ed Miliband's Labour government in the event of a hung Parliament.

The Grays wrote that their daughter now lives in constant fear of black men, but Judge Stevens accused them of fostering racist stereotypes, suggesting the mother had "attributed her own views to her child," while granting Wallace probation despite the family's belief that "if holding a little girl at gunpoint gets you probation, then our system is flawed." The controversial ruling sparked outrage among the family and community members who called for Stevens' removal, reports indicate.

Mysterious cardboard signs bearing messages like "please forgive me" and "we need to talk" have appeared across the affluent Surrey village of Milford, sparking speculation among residents about the identity of the author and the reasons behind this contrite gesture. The signs, which express deep love and profess the recipient as the love of their life, represent a desperate and unconventional approach to seeking reconciliation.

They now face potential jail sentences of up to five years if formally charged, with prosecutors set to decide whether to formally arrest, release, or return their cases to police.

Defence Force Chief Mark Binskin described the horror the Anzacs faced as the quiet dawn and gentle waves gave way to the roar of gunfire and cries of the wounded, noting that for many, the rising sun that day would be their last. The ceremony honored the thousands of soldiers who fought and fell during the eight-month campaign, celebrating their sacrifice as founding heroes of modern Australia and New Zealand.

Torres has found goals difficult to come by since his January return to Atletico Madrid, with Antoine Griezmann scoring twice to salvage a draw for the champions.
